meaning: The equal treatment of all citizens without any social distinction based on caste, color, race, religion, or sex. It ensures the elimination of social privileges and the improvement of conditions for weaker sections like SCs, STs, and women.

The non-discrimination between people on the basis of economic factors. It aims at the equitable distribution of wealth, resources, and means of production, preventing the concentration of economic power in a few hands.

A common set of personal laws governing all citizens regardless of their religion, covering areas like marriage, divorce, inheritance, adoption, and succession across the entire country under Article 44.

An income level under Article 43 that enables a worker to secure not just basic necessities (food, clothing, shelter), but also education, healthcare, social security, and a decent standard of life with leisure.
A system of rural local self-government in India, envisioned under Article 40 to decentralize power and empower village assemblies as fundamental units of democratic governance.
The constitutional directive under Article 48A and Fundamental Duty under Article 51A(g) mandating the State and citizens to protect, improve, and safeguard forests, wildlife, and natural ecosystems.

A set of moral and civic obligations enlisted under Part IVA (Article 51A) that remind citizens to uphold national integrity, respect national symbols, and contribute to social harmony while enjoying fundamental rights.
Adherence to the core values, democratic principles, and constitutional philosophy rather than mere legal literalism, ensuring harmony between state actions, citizen duties, and fundamental rights.
